title: TabPFN-Wide: Continued Pre-Training for Extreme Feature Counts
authors: Christopher Kolberg, Jules Kreuer, Jonas Huurdeman, Sofiane Ouaari (equal contribution); Katharina Eggensperger; Nico Pfeifer (corresponding). University of Tübingen (Institute for Bioinformatics & Medical Informatics; AutoML for Science) + Lamarr Institute / TU Dortmund.
venue: arXiv:2510.06162 [cs.LG], submitted Oct 7 2025, revised Mar 29 2026 (v2). Journal-formatted preprint (template suggests a bioinformatics journal submission); not yet confirmed peer-reviewed at this version.
tokenization_method: Does NOT change the tokenizer. It keeps TabPFNv2's per-CELL tokenization and two-axis (feature-axis + sample-axis) dual attention unchanged, and instead ADAPTS THE WEIGHTS by continued pre-training on a new synthetic prior that produces extreme-feature-count datasets. The novel contribution is a 'feature widening' prior: start from a moderate-width synthetic dataset (50-350 features) sampled from the open-source TabICL SCM prior, then synthetically inflate (widen) it to a target width d (up to dmax in {1500, 5000, 8000}) before feeding it as an in-context training task. Continued pre-training updates ALL TabPFNv2 classifier parameters (AdamW, lr 1e-5, weight decay 1e-4, batch 16/8, 10,000 steps) so the frozen-at-inference PFN learns to do in-context Bayesian prediction over thousands of feature-tokens. Critically, for interpretability they DISABLE TabPFNv2's default feature grouping / distribution-dependent extra-feature addition / feature removal so that each transformer feature-token maps 1:1 to one dataset feature.
feature_types_handled: numerical (continuous) and categorical, mixed; missing values inherited from TabPFNv2 handling. The widening prior treats a feature as categorical if it has <=20 distinct values, else continuous, and widens each type with a dedicated algorithm. NO native temporal/time-varying axis (each row is a static vector); time-series omics not addressed.
token_granularity: per-feature, strictly. One token = one (sample, feature) cell, and they intentionally enforce a 1:1 token-to-feature correspondence (disabling TabPFNv2's cell-grouping) so that feature-axis attention scores can be read as per-feature importances. This is the OPPOSITE design choice from TabPFN-2.5, which GROUPS feature-cells (groups of 3) to compress the feature axis for scale.
numerical_strategy: Inherited from TabPFNv2 (raw values + internal normalization, no hand-designed numerical embedding). The novelty is on the PRIOR side: continuous widening (Algorithm 1) creates new continuous features as sparse random linear projections of existing features X_wide = X (M ⊙ W), W~N(0,1), mask M~Bernoulli(p), then adds feature-dependent Gaussian noise N~N(0, σ·σ_j) where σ_j = std of the projected column. Sparsity p and noise σ are sampled per dataset (p in [0,0.05], σ in [0,1]) to match dense/sparse correlation structures seen in biomedical data.
categorical_strategy: Inherited TabPFNv2 categorical handling at inference. Novelty in the prior: categorical widening (Algorithm 2) builds each new categorical column by sparsely sampling k=max(1, floor(p·m_cat)) donor categorical columns, copying values per-sample from a randomly chosen donor, then a category-reduction step merges rarest categories until cardinality <= K_j, where target cardinality K_j is drawn from a discrete exponential distribution (biased low, occasionally high).
high_cardinality_handling: Explicitly BOUNDED, not enabled: the categorical widening prior caps cardinality (K_j sampled from a discrete exponential biased toward low cardinality, 3..K_max) to 'prevent degenerate high-cardinality variables.' So the model is trained for MANY low-cardinality categorical columns (SNP/genotype-style), not for extreme-cardinality text/ID fields. High-cardinality categoricals remain unaddressed.
temporal_handling: none. No time-varying features, no patching/recurrence. The omics setting concatenates static multi-omics modalities along the feature axis (early integration).
scales_to_many_features: This is the headline contribution and it is strong WITHIN the HDLSS regime: the model 'seamlessly scales beyond 30,000 categorical and continuous features.' Real omics (TCGA, early-integrated multi-omics) reach up to ~60,000 features; synthetic SNP needle-in-haystack reaches up to ~70,000 categorical features. Performance stays stable as feature count grows (Fig 3, 4b) while TabPFNv2/TabICL collapse toward random. BUT the validated setting is high-dimensional LOW-sample-size (m >> n; n typically tens to a few hundred). Cost is unchanged-architecture: still (Flash-)attention with quadratic complexity and high memory; widening during training raises complexity quadratically with d, which is why dmax was capped at 8k and 1.5k=5k=8k variants gave near-identical downstream accuracy. Inference runtime complexity 'remains unaffected' vs TabPFNv2; continued pre-training used 4xH100 (320GB).
downstream_model: The transformer IS the model: TabPFNv2 classifier checkpoint, frozen at inference, in-context prediction in one forward pass (no per-dataset training). TabPFN-Wide is the same architecture with continued-pre-trained weights. Only the classifier is adapted; the regressor is left as future work.
pretraining_objective: Continued pre-training (sequential further training of an existing checkpoint) with cross-entropy loss on PURELY synthetic in-context classification tasks produced by the feature-widening prior. The objective is to acquire the extreme-feature-count regime WITHOUT catastrophic forgetting of the original <=500-feature regime. With prob 0.5 the original (un-widened) features are also appended then the column order is randomly permuted, to keep permutation-invariance and anchor the original distribution.
key_results: (1) Real omics: best AUROC across 5 TCGA multi-omics cancer-subtype datasets; TabPFNv2 and TabICL underperform because untrained for extreme widths; tree methods stable but lower. (2) No catastrophic forgetting: on 21 TabArena tasks (<=10k samples, <=500 features) TabPFN-Wide(5k) tracks TabPFNv2 almost exactly (Spearman rho=0.9935). (3) Needle-in-a-haystack SNP: as non-causal SNPs grow to ~70k features, TabPFN-Wide and XGBoost degrade least; TabICL collapses to AUROC~0.5. (4) 15 HDLSS datasets (Li et al. 2018): TabPFN-Wide 92.0% pairwise win rate vs RF 68%, RealMLP 56%, XGBoost 44%, TabPFNv2 34.7%, TabICL 5.3%; paired Wilcoxon p<0.005. (5) Interpretability: on controlled synthetic signal-recovery, attention-to-label Recall@k and noise suppression on par with Random Forest impurity importance; on TCGA mRNA, top-10 attention genes overlap known cancer biomarkers (e.g. 9/10 in BRCA). (6) Training width 1.5k vs 5k vs 8k barely changes downstream accuracy -> 5k chosen as default.
baselines_compared: TabPFNv2, TabICL (foundation models); RealMLP-TD (pre-tuned NN); Random Forest; XGBoost (tree-based). Ensembling deliberately disabled for the foundation models, RealMLP-TD, and TabPFN-Wide to study raw model behaviour.
transferable_idea: Prior-engineering + continued pre-training as a CHEAP way to extend a frozen tabular foundation model into a new feature-count regime WITHOUT retraining from scratch and WITHOUT changing the tokenizer/architecture. For the 70+-heterogeneous-feature target the directly reusable recipe is: (a) take a strong pretrained tabular PFN whose tokenizer is already permutation-invariant per-feature; (b) design a synthetic 'widening' prior that inflates moderate-width SCM datasets into the target width via sparse random linear projection (continuous) + sparse donor-copy-then-cardinality-cap (categorical) with feature-dependent noise, controlled by a sparsity knob p that tunes inter-feature correlation; (c) continue pre-training all params for a fixed budget with the original distribution mixed back in (prob 0.5) to avoid catastrophic forgetting. Secondary transferable idea: keep a strict 1:1 token-to-feature mapping (disable cell grouping/derived features) when per-feature interpretability matters, and read averaged feature-axis attention-to-label as an importance proxy.
limitations: (1) Validated ONLY for HDLSS (m >> n) and only for the TabPFNv2 CLASSIFIER; regressor untested. (2) Architecture unchanged -> still bound by quadratic (Flash-)attention compute and memory; cannot also scale samples; widening raises training cost quadratically in d (dmax capped at 8k). (3) The same widening prior FAILED to train TabICL ('initial attempts unsuccessful') -> unclear if it is a prior problem or TabICL architecture limitation. (4) High-cardinality categoricals deliberately bounded out of the prior. (5) Attention-as-importance is acknowledged as controversial; clean only on synthetic ground-truth, 'interpret with caution' on real biomedical data; no systematic eval. (6) Categorical-vs-continuous split uses a crude <=20-distinct-values heuristic. (7) Needs the 1:1-token mapping mode, which disables TabPFNv2's own feature-grouping speedups.
pitfalls: (1) 'Scales beyond 30,000 features' is true ONLY in the LOW-SAMPLE regime (tens-to-hundreds of rows) and for nearly-categorical/correlated omics-style data; it is NOT a claim about many-rows wide data, and the widening prior bakes in HDLSS assumptions (high inter-feature correlation + noise) that may not hold for a 70-feature mixed-temporal-static business table with many rows. (2) The strong robustness is partly a function of the synthetic-signal needle-in-haystack construction; real-omics gains rest on 5 datasets x 5-fold CV (small n). (3) Attention scores are presented as interpretability but the authors themselves cite 'attention is not explanation' literature; biomarker overlap is suggestive, not causal validation. (4) Direct design TENSION with TabPFN-2.5: TabPFN-Wide DISABLES feature grouping to keep per-feature tokens, whereas TabPFN-2.5 ADDS feature grouping (groups of 3) to scale; the two scaling philosophies (prior-side adaptation vs token-compression) are not jointly tested. (5) Only the classifier; regression for HDLSS is open.
relevance_to_topic: Demonstrates that the way to make a permutation-invariant per-feature tabular tokenizer handle FAR more features (30k-70k) need not be a new tokenizer at all — it can be continued pre-training on a feature-widening synthetic prior, which is a concrete, low-cost transfer recipe for pushing any pretrained per-feature tokenizer past its training feature ceiling while keeping per-feature interpretability.
code_available: yes — training code, package, and model weights released at https://github.com/not-a-feature/TabPFN-Wide (base checkpoint is the TabPFNv2 classifier on Hugging Face). Reproducibility of the prior is good (both widening algorithms given as pseudocode); usual TabPFN non-commercial license caveats apply to the base weights.
